findings.evidence: PowMonoidHom.lean:57 `private theorem square_eq_powMonoidHom_two_range ... Subgroup.square G = (powMonoidHom 2 : G →* G).range`; the new file's `hrange` states the same goal.
findings.fix: Drop `private` on square_eq_powMonoidHom_two_range, `import TauCeti.Algebra.Group.PowMonoidHom`, and use it for `hrange` instead of re-proving.
findings.issue: Inside card_elementaryTwoQuotient_eq_card_sq_eq_one, the `hrange` step proves `Subgroup.square G = (powMonoidHom 2).range`, identical to the existing `TauCeti.square_eq_powMonoidHom_two_range` in TauCeti/Algebra/Group/PowMonoidHom.lean (same directory), currently `private`.

findings.evidence: SquareClassGroup.lean:33 `abbrev SquareClassGroup ... := Additive Kˣ ⧸ (Subgroup.square Kˣ).toAddSubgroup`; the PR's own `hsq` proves this subgroup equals `range (lsmul ℤ _ 2)`, i.e. the same quotient as `ModN (Additive Kˣ) 2`.
findings.fix: Add a transfer `SquareClassGroup K ≃ₗ[ZMod 2] TauCeti.elementaryTwoQuotient Kˣ` (it can live in SquareClassGroup.lean, which may import Algebra/Group), even if the full redirect of SquareClassGroup is left to the signposted follow-up.
findings.issue: `TauCeti.elementaryTwoQuotient Kˣ` is the same object as the pre-existing `TauCeti.SquareClassGroup K` (Kˣ⧸(Kˣ)²), which the new general construction now subsumes; the two are parallel APIs with no equivalence linking them, so downstream code over Kˣ has two square-class objects and no transfer lemma.

summary: The new general construction reuses Mathlib's ModN well, but the inline hrange re-proves the existing private lemma TauCeti.square_eq_powMonoidHom_two_range, and elementaryTwoQuotient Kˣ is a parallel API to the pre-existing SquareClassGroup with no transfer lemma connecting them.
